mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and residential investment demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive Sharpe ratio observed in the subsequent 20-day window across rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation months, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak nonfarm payrolls growth below 100 thousand signals deteriorating labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: Markets price in weak payroll data as a precursor to rate cuts, resulting in positive forward returns in greater than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth outpaces retail sales growth indicates rising business investment and export demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS spread fails to predict returns, with no statistical edge observed in out-of-sample quarterly forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce short-term financing costs for leveraged buyouts and margin trading, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS levels show no predictive power for 5-day returns, with a mean forward return indistinguishable from zero across all tested periods.
